titleStmt.title: Letter from Fr William Keary to Fr Provincial Thomas V. Nolan, 6 October 1918
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: W. Keary

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Fr William Keary (1881-1958) to Fr Provincial Thomas V Nolan (1867-1941) regarding the news Fr John Fitzgibbon's death. Fr. Thomas V Nolan was the Provincial of the Irish Province of the Society of Jesus (1912-1922) and was a member of the Distribution Committee which looked after the welfare and distribution of the Belgian refugees who arrived in Ireland as a consequence of the First World War.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Sunday 6-10-18 Dear Fr. Provinical P.C.
Many thanks for your kind letter.
I was on leave when Fr. Fitzgibbon was killed, and
when I returned I found that his effects had
all been sent to his home address (to his father).
He was so much loved and respected that great
care was taken that all (clothes, altar-kit, etc.) was sent.
It must indeed be a great loss to your Reverence;
for I cannot tell you what a loss he is to me.
Apart from out intimate friendship, he was such a
splendid organiser, had so tactful a way, and was so
zealous and hardworking; and yet was so kindly and
encouraging to us younger Chaplains that I feel quite
'at sea' without him. May he rest in peace.I have
said 4 Masses for his soul, and hope to say another soon.
Mass is a sore treat in the rough and tumble life
we are leading during the present advance. Yours very respectfully in Christ, W. Keary S.J. 16 Field Ambulance, B.E.F.
